justification: Brief is clearly scoped to Sprint 1 only (scope field, task_context, out_of_scope section). Sprint 2 retry logic is explicitly excluded with direct instruction ('No retry logic in this sprint — that is explicitly deferred to Sprint 2'). All three original ACs are present plus a bonus timeout AC (AC4). 5-second timeout is included as a hard requirement. MJ's 'leave extension points' guidance is passed through as architecture_guidance with explicit note not to add retry loops, BullMQ, or backoff logic. No retry logic appears in implementation guidance.

observations.text: Scope field immediately declares 'Sprint 1 only — do NOT implement Sprint 2 items'
All three ACs present (POST with JSON, 2xx = delivered, non-2xx = failed) plus a fourth timeout AC that strengthens coverage
5-second timeout explicitly called out as hard requirement in both domain_rules and architecture_guidance
MJ's extension points guidance faithfully passed through: 'Leave extension points for retry but do not implement retry' with clear boundary — comments OK, code is not
out_of_scope section enumerates all Sprint 2 items (RetryScheduler, BullMQ, backoff, suspension)
Domain rules have testable assertions making them directly actionable for Shaq
Thoughtful open_questions that surface genuine ambiguities without blocking Sprint 1 work
terminology_alignment section reduces risk of domain/code vocabulary drift

agent_output_excerpt:
```yaml
handoff_brief:
  recipient: Shaq
  scope: Sprint 1 only — do NOT implement Sprint 2 items

  task_context: >
    Build the basic webhook delivery service. When a webhook event occurs, the system
    must HTTP POST the event payload (as JSON) to a subscriber's registered URL, wait
    up to 5 seconds for a response, and record whether delivery succeeded or failed
    based on the HTTP status code returned. No retry logic in this sprint — that is
    explicitly deferred to Sprint 2.

  dom
